overview.short: The Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Sidhi district of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: The Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in the Sidhi district of Madhya Pradesh. It was declared a wildlife sanctuary in 1981 by the State government for the conservation of gharials.

topPlaces.name: Chandreh
topPlaces.description: Chandreh, also spelled Chandrehi or Chandrehe, is a village in Rampur Naikin block of Sidhi district, Madhya Pradesh. Located near the confluence of the Son and Banas rivers, it is known for its 10th-century Shiva temple and monastery.

items.note: Chandreh, also spelled Chandrehi or Chandrehe, is a village in Rampur Naikin block of Sidhi district, Madhya Pradesh. Located near the confluence of the Son and Banas rivers, it is known for its 10th-century Shiva temple and monastery.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Prayagraj Airport (Bamrauli) (132 km) and the nearest railway station is Satna Junction (72 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Son Gharial Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
